methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $3.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $183.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Anthropic
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series G), post-money $183B

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series G), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Together AI, a Series G AI & Software company founded by Vipul Prakash and Ce Zhang, is valued at $3.3B post-money on $534M total raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables spanning Anthropic ($183B), Waymo ($126B), Applied Intuition ($15B), Dataminr ($4.1B), and Labelbox ($1.0B), alongside one public reference point (Palantir) and additional private benchmarks.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.25x step-up multiplier to the $3.3B last post-money valuation, implying $4.1B. Peer-median analysis across the five-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $15.0B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $183.0B. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the AI software space; Anthropic's $183B valuation significantly exceeds market norms for this stage.

The triangulated range—$3.5B (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $9.6B—reflects this methodological divergence. Stage-progression anchors near the current valuation, while peer-median points substantially higher. Key uncertainties include the appropriate comparable set composition given extreme valuation variance within generative AI, and whether Together AI's business profile aligns more closely with infrastructure-layer peers or foundation-model participants. The $9.6B midpoint carries material sensitivity to peer selection and stage-progression assumptions.
